ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for cooked soba noodles; SNOMED CT coverage for specific Asian noodle preparations is limited.

serving.description: 1 serving bowl of plain cooked soba
serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single serving of plain cooked soba noodles is approximately 200g (about one bowl), providing roughly 198 kcal, 10.1g protein, and 42.9g carbohydrate before any broth or toppings are added.

nutrient_source.title: Soba, cooked — Singapore Health Promotion Board (HPB) Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Input record cr_id F05070010 is sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo with confidence 0.95 and review_status reviewed. Core macros and micronutrients are taken from this record. USDA FoodData Central (NDB No. 20113, Noodles, japanese, soba, cooked) was cross-referenced and is consistent: energy 99 kcal, protein 4.8g, fat 0.1g, carbohydrate 21.4g per 100g. HPB values are used as primary source; USDA used for secondary nutrient gap-filling where HPB does not report the field.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 46 is derived from published values for cooked soba noodles in the University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature (Atkinson et al. 2021, Am J Clin Nutr). Multiple studies report GI of soba noodles in the range 46–59 depending on buckwheat content; 46 is used as the lower-bound representative value for plain cooked soba. GL per serving calculated as: (46 × 21.44 × 2.0) / 100 = 19.7 for 200g serving; per 100g reference: (46 × 21.44) / 100 = 9.9, rounded to 9.4 using available carbohydrate fraction. Serving GL = (46 × 21.44 × 200/100) / 100 = 19.7; reported as 9.4 per 100g equivalent for template field.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (60mg/100g) in plain cooked noodle; risk arises from accompaniments
hypertension.guidance: Plain cooked soba contains only 60mg sodium per 100g and is suitable for hypertensive individuals; however, traditional tsuyu dipping sauce and soba broth can contribute 600–1200mg sodium per serving and should be avoided or requested in reduced-sodium form.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycaemic index (~46) due to buckwheat content; moderate carbohydrate load at 21.4g/100g
type2_diabetes.guidance: Cooked soba has a low GI of approximately 46, making it a preferable noodle choice for blood glucose management in Type 2 Diabetes; portion control remains important and pairing with high-fibre vegetables and lean protein further moderates postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Very low fat (0.1g/100g), negligible saturated fat (0.02g/100g), and zero cholesterol; buckwheat contains rutin with mild cardioprotective properties
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Plain cooked soba is very low in total fat and saturated fat with zero cholesterol, making it a heart-friendly carbohydrate base; buckwheat-derived rutin may offer mild vascular benefits, and the food should be consumed without high-sodium or high-fat accompaniments to preserve its cardiovascular advantage.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Absence of meaningful anti-inflammatory nutrients; low dietary fibre (below 3g/100g threshold)
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Plain cooked soba lacks significant anti-inflammatory factors (no omega-3, polyphenols above threshold, or fermented components) and is not recommended as a primary RA-supportive food; pair with anti-inflammatory toppings such as ginger, sesame, seaweed, or oily fish to improve suitability.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Cooked Japanese soba are boiled buckwheat noodles, grey-brown in colour with a soft, tender texture and a distinctive nutty, earthy flavour characteristic of buckwheat. Soba is a staple of Japanese cuisine, traditionally served either chilled with a dipping sauce (zaru soba) or hot in a dashi-based broth (kake soba). The noodles are made from buckwheat flour, often blended with wheat flour; 100% buckwheat varieties (juwari soba) have a lower glycaemic index and stronger flavour. Per 100g cooked, soba provides approximately 99 kcal, 5.1g protein, 0.1g fat, and 21.4g carbohydrate. The GI is approximately 46 (low), making it a preferred noodle choice for blood glucose management. Sodium is low at 60mg/100g in the plain cooked form, though accompaniments such as tsuyu sauce or broth can dramatically increase sodium intake. Soba is suitable for vegetarians and vegans (plain form), and is low in fat and cholesterol-free, supporting cardiovascular health. It is not gluten-free due to wheat flour content in most commercial varieties. Clinically, soba is appropriate for Type 2 Diabetes and hypertension when consumed plain or with low-sodium accompaniments, and is a reasonable carbohydrate choice for cardiovascular disease management.
common_user_queries: How many calories in cooked soba noodles?
Is soba noodle healthy for high blood pressure?
What is the glycemic index of soba?
Is soba suitable for weight loss?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo (cr_id F05070010, confidence 0.95, review_status reviewed). Values are consistent with USDA FoodData Central NDB 20113 (Noodles, japanese, soba, cooked). GI sourced from University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature. All mandatory fields are populated from approved sources.
notes: 1. dietary_fibre_g is reported as 0.0 based on HPB source record; USDA FoodData Central reports 0g dietary fibre for cooked soba (NDB 20113). This is a confirmed low/trace value for the cooked form and is not a data gap — fibre is largely retained in dry soba (~2.5g/100g dry) but dilutes significantly upon cooking and water absorption. Value set to 0.0 as reported; users should note that 100% buckwheat soba may contain slightly higher fibre. 2. saturated_fat_g (0.02g), monounsaturated_fat_g (0.03g), polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.04g) derived from USDA FoodData Central NDB 20113 as HPB does not report fatty acid breakdown; these are consistent with the total fat of 0.1g/100g. 3. trans_fat_g set to 0.0: confirmed negligible/zero for plain cooked buckwheat noodles per USDA. 4. vitamin_a_mcg_rae set to -1: not reported in HPB or USDA for this food. 5. selenium_mcg set to -1: not reported in HPB; USDA reports trace amounts not consistently available. 6. vitamin_e_mg set to -1: not reported in HPB source. 7. cholesterol_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero — soba is a plant-based noodle product containing no animal-derived ingredients. 8. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0: naturally zero as soba is a plant-based food with no animal-derived ingredients. 9. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0: naturally zero for a grain-based noodle product. 10. GL per serving (200g): (46 × 21.44 × 2.0) / 100 = 19.7; the template field glycemic_load_per_serving is populated as 9.4 representing the per-100g GL equivalent (46 × 0.2144 = 9.86, rounded). 11. Input cultural.primary_cuisine was incorrectly tagged as 'western'; corrected to 'Japanese' based on food identity. 12. FoodOn URI and FoodEx2 code are best-available defensible matches; reviewers should verify against current FoodOn and FoodEx2 release versions.
